CVE-2025-7458.8e678644a76ec12be9c07098cad777ebfb771d1a:
Overhaul Docker base images and release workflows (#18178)

* Adding more github action tasks

* Remove unused openjdk.dockerfile base images

The upstream docker.io/openjdk image is deprecated. The openjdk.dockerfile
files were not referenced by any workflow or script (matrix only uses
amazoncorretto and ms-openjdk). Replacing them with mcr.microsoft.com/openjdk/jdk
would duplicate ms-openjdk.dockerfile, so delete them instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Upgrade Docker base images and build tooling

- Migrate amazoncorretto build image from debian:buster-slim (EOL) to
  debian:bookworm-slim; update Corretto GPG key import to modern
  gpg --dearmor approach
- Migrate amazoncorretto runtime image from al2-jdk to al2023-jdk
  (Amazon Linux 2 is EOL)
- Migrate ms-openjdk runtime base from ubuntu:24.10 (non-LTS) to
  ubuntu:24.04 LTS
- Replace libboost-all-dev with libboost-dev in all build images to
  reduce image size
- Upgrade Maven from 3.9.9 to 3.9.14 in all build images
- Add make -j$(nproc) for parallel Thrift compilation; clean up
  temp build artifacts
- Make JDK version dynamic (ARG JAVA_VERSION) in amazoncorretto
  build image; expand base-image build matrix to JDK 11/17/21/25
- Add manifest push job to build-pinot-base-docker-image workflow
- Add Java 25 to build-multi-arch-pinot-docker-image matrix
- Add arm64 support to superset docker build script
- Change default manifest tag from 11-amazoncorretto to
  17-amazoncorretto
- Add -ntp flag to Maven build in Dockerfile.build
- Pin build stage platform to linux/amd64 in Dockerfile.package
- Remove unnecessary packages (vim, python-is-python3, linux-perf)
  from runtime images

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add nightly snapshot, on-demand release, and base image release workflows

- nightly-build-all-pinot-docker-image.yml: fires at 23:00 UTC daily
  (workflow_dispatch also available); builds all JDK 11/17/21/25 x
  amazoncorretto/ms-openjdk variants as multi-arch amd64+arm64 images
  and pushes to apachepinot/pinot on DockerHub

- build-all-pinot-docker-image.yml: on-demand release workflow with
  inputs for git URL, branch/tag/commit, image name, and tags;
  same JDK matrix as the nightly workflow

- .pinot_compile_and_push_build_image.sh: compiles Pinot on amd64 from
  source and pushes the build image to DockerHub; includes the commit
  SHA in the image tag to prevent collisions between concurrent runs

- .pinot_package_single_platform.sh: pulls the commit-scoped build
  image and produces a platform-specific package image for amd64 or arm64

- .pinot_base_docker_manifest_build_and_push.sh: creates and pushes the
  multi-arch manifest for pinot-base-build and pinot-base-runtime images;
  idempotent (removes stale local manifest before re-creating)

All three matrix jobs use fail-fast: false so a single JDK variant
failure does not cancel the other variants.

Note: 17-amazoncorretto is now the canonical runtime for the bare
`latest` tag (previously 11-amazoncorretto), matching the default
JDK version bump in .pinot_multi_arch_docker_image_manifest_package.sh.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Optimize compile matrix and clean up Docker build variables

- Reduce compile-pinot jobs from 8 to 4 by removing java-disto from the
  compile matrix; JVM bytecode is vendor-neutral so one build image per
  JDK version is shared by all runtime distros (amazoncorretto, ms-openjdk)
- Rename PINOT_BRANCH to PINOT_GIT_REF to reflect that the value can be
  a branch, tag, commit SHA, or release ref
- Remove PINOT_GIT_URL everywhere; hardcode https://github.com/apache/pinot.git
  since this is always the Apache Pinot repo for official builds
- Update KAFKA_VERSION default from 2.0 to 3.0 in docker-build.sh

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Harden base images: upgrade OS packages, Thrift 0.22.0, remove git from runtime

- Run apt-get upgrade --no-install-recommends in both build images and
  ms-openjdk runtime to apply all OS-level security patches at build time
- Upgrade Thrift compiler 0.12.0 → 0.22.0 to match libthrift version in
  Pinot's pom.xml and eliminate known CVEs in the old Thrift binary
- Verify Thrift tarball integrity via SHA-512 checksum before extraction
- Remove git from both runtime images (not needed at runtime; reduces
  attack surface)
- Add set -eux to ms-openjdk build RUN for consistent fail-fast behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Reduce Corretto runtime CVE surface: drop gpg, use apt-native .asc key

Replace gpg --dearmor key handling with apt's native ASCII-armored key
support: save corretto.key directly as /etc/apt/keyrings/corretto.asc
(apt reads .asc files natively via signed-by= from that path). This
removes the gpg/gpgconf/libreadline/libsqlite3 dependency chain,
eliminating the libsqlite3-0 CRITICAL CVE (CVE-2025-7458) from both
pinot-base-runtime and pinot-base-build amazoncorretto images.

Also remove fontconfig from runtime prereqs (not needed for headless
server) and add libtasn1-6 explicitly (used by TLS/X.509 cert chains).
Add ENV PATH="${JAVA_HOME}/bin:${PATH}" to runtime Dockerfile to match
ms-openjdk pattern. Fix ARG JAVA_VERSION=11 default in post-FROM
declarations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add [Pinot Release] prefix to Docker release workflow names

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR review comments

- Remove nightly-pull-upstream-master-branch.yml: fork-specific sync
  workflow using personal token; does not belong in apache/pinot
- Fix --platform amd64 → linux/amd64 in compile script
- Fix git clone -b <ref> to clone + checkout so commit SHAs work
  (in .pinot_build_info_gen.sh and .pinot_docker_image_build_and_push.sh)
- Fix PINOT_GIT_REF: use github.sha instead of env.GITHUB_REF in
  pinot_vuln_check.yml (env context does not expose runner GITHUB_REF)
- Remove stale OPENJDK_IMAGE build-arg from README arm64 examples
  (Dockerfile no longer accepts OPENJDK_IMAGE)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix docker build --push to docker buildx build in superset workflow

--push is a buildx flag; use docker buildx build explicitly to avoid
ambiguity even when setup-buildx-action configures the default builder.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove unused Docker workflows and scripts

- build-superset-docker-image-new.yml: byte-for-byte duplicate of
  build-superset-docker-image.yml
- .pinot_all_platforms_docker_image_build.sh: dead code, no workflow
  references it; still uses removed PINOT_GIT_URL/PINOT_BRANCH vars
  and invalid --platform amd64 values
- .superset_multi_arch_docker_image_build_and_push.sh: dead code, no
  workflow references it; the nightly superset workflow uses inline
  shell steps instead

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
